title: Run contract (v1)
description: Canonical v1 schema for an agent run contract: the single typed envelope that binds trigger, model policy, context, capabilities, authority, hooks, verifiers and outputs into a unit of execution. Every agent run MUST be a run-contract instance.

name.description: Kebab-case identifier; doubles as audit-log scope.

schema_version.description: Semver of this run-contract schema. v1 contracts MUST declare a 1.x.y string.

fingerprint.description: sha256:<hex> content-hash of the canonicalised contract body. Generated; not author-supplied.

description.description: One-sentence human-readable summary of what this contract authorises.

verifiers.description: Closed set of verifier-pack ids (ADR-0004) to run post-execution. An empty array is loud-not-silent: it explicitly declares NO post-run verification. Validators MAY still reject empty arrays at policy level.

trigger.description: What causes this contract to be instantiated. Every triggered run compiles down to a single typed shape. Each oneOf branch fixes its own required and additionalProperties, so the outer object intentionally does not.

schedule.description: Cron expression in the executor's local convention (5-field or 6-field with seconds).

timezone.description: IANA timezone for the schedule (defaults to UTC if absent).

jitter_seconds.description: Random jitter window added to the scheduled fire time.

endpoint.description: URL path the webhook handler listens on.

signature_scheme.description: Signature scheme the sender must use. `none` is loud-not-silent — it makes the absence explicit.

shared_secret_ref.description: Reference (NOT the secret itself) to the verification material, e.g. `secret://provider/payments/hmac`.

branch_filter.description: Optional list of branch globs that gate execution.

paths.description: Glob patterns inside the filesystem root that trigger the contract.

from_allow_list.description: From addresses or domain globs permitted to trigger.

subject_filter.description: Optional regex against the subject line.

operator_id.description: Operator identifier required to authorise the run.

approval_required.description: Whether a separate approval step gates the run beyond the operator id.

model_policy.description: Closed model allow-list plus explicit deny-list. The deny-list is load-bearing: Stage 2 evidence found narrow-scope model-class confabulation on SKILLS access.

allowed_models.description: Models permitted to execute this contract. Empty arrays are rejected.

disallow_models.description: Models that MUST be denied even if generally available. Explicit redundancy for safety.

fallback.description: What to do when no allowed_model is available. Either a model id to substitute, or the literal string `fail` (loud-not-silent default). Validators MAY warn if neither a known model id nor `fail`.

context.description: Skills surfaced to the agent and memory access policy.

skills.description: Skill ids made available. Per ADR-0004, every cited skill is REQUIRED BY CONVENTION to have a sibling verifier-pack. NOTE: this JSON Schema and scripts/validate-contracts-v1.py validate shape only -- on-disk sibling existence is NOT checked here; that gate, if present, lives in external catalog tooling.

mode.description: verified_only = only operator-signed memories; candidate_ok = pending memories surface; off = no memory.

max_items.description: Cap on retrieved memory items per query.

ceiling.description: memory-service truth-ceiling lattice level. RemoteSigned is the strongest.

capabilities.description: Closed tool set. Anything outside is denied at the host-policy-layer boundary.

tools.description: MCP tool ids. Empty array is legal but means the agent has no tools — verifiers will check outputs anyway.

authority.description: Bounded authority surface for the run. Filesystem, env, network and subprocess are the four axes; each defaults to typed denial.

root.description: Absolute path: all filesystem operations are confined here.

writable.description: Either a boolean (whole root) or a list of relative paths permitted for writes. Defaults to false (read-only) if absent.

readable.description: Optional explicit read-allow list. If omitted, the entire root is readable.

inherit.description: Whether to inherit parent env. Default false (typed denial).

allow.description: Explicit env var allow-list. Names only — values come from the resolved environment.

deny.description: Explicit env var deny-list / glob patterns.

mode.description: deny = no outbound network; allow_list = only listed hosts. There is intentionally no `allow_all` mode.

allow_list.description: Required when mode == allow_list. host:port patterns.

allowed.description: Either a boolean (all/none) or a closed list of binary paths (absolute) the run may exec.

hooks.description: Lifecycle hook map. Each key is a hook name from the canonical 13-hook list; each value is an ordered array of hook-action ids.

outputs.description: Declared artefacts the verifiers will check. The `required` list is the close on the Stage 2 silent-stub class: an output declared as required that does not exist post-run fails the contract loudly.

required.description: Path patterns that MUST exist after the run. Empty is rejected at validation; even research-only runs declare an audit log path.

optional.description: Path patterns that MAY exist; absence is not a failure.

jsonl_path.description: Relative path inside the filesystem root for the JSONL audit log.

content_addressable.description: Whether artefact references in the audit log are content-hashed.
